notes: The login form at /login and the happy path (valid credentials) work correctly — POST /session with valid creds returns 200 with Set-Cookie and Sign out link.
The regression is that credential validation is skipped: any non-empty identifier/secret bypasses auth and gets a valid session cookie.
Empty credentials (identifier=&secret=) still correctly return 401 with error message.

observations.value: POST /session with invalid credentials (e.g., identifier=bad@user.com&secret=wrongpassword, or identifier=nobody&secret=incorrect, or identifier=x&secret=y) returns HTTP 200 with Set-Cookie: session=ok and a Home page containing 'Sign out', instead of the documented HTTP 401 with an inline error. Authentication is effectively bypassed for any non-empty credential pair. Only completely empty identifier and secret fields trigger the expected 401 'Invalid credentials' response.

detections.observation_text: POST /session with invalid credentials (e.g., identifier=bad@user.com&secret=wrongpassword, or identifier=nobody&secret=incorrect, or identifier=x&secret=y) returns HTTP 200 with Set-Cookie: session=ok and a Home page containing 'Sign out', instead of the documented HTTP 401 with an inline error. Authentication is effectively bypassed for any non-empty credential pair. Only completely empty identifier and secret fields trigger the expected 401 'Invalid credentials' response.

judge_verdict.label: false_positive
judge_verdict.reason: The observation describes invalid credentials being accepted (auth bypass), which is not listed in the manifest; the closest entry t1_login_500 expects a 500 error on login, and s1_oracle_lies expects a 401 on a valid session, neither of which matches the reported behavior of 200 with Set-Cookie on bad credentials.
